data.narrative:
Armada is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Dan Wright and Jon Runyan that has raised $186M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, with 3 direct same-stage comparables identified: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $186M raised proxy, implying $353M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $400M valuation, with the peer set ranging from $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent revenue disclosure; the sector median P/S across 6 public tickers is 49.8x, but this method requires target-specific revenue data.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $300M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $377M. This range reflects material dispersion in peer valuations—the 1.3B Aalyria multiple significantly exceeds the peer median—introducing uncertainty around whether Armada commands a premium or discount relative to the broader peer set. Key unknowns include revenue scale, growth trajectory, and specific technical differentiation relative to named comparables.
